name: What kind of warranties come with new window installations?
headline: What kind of warranties come with new window installations?

mainEntity.name: What kind of warranties usually come with new window installations?
mainEntity.text: What kind of warranties usually come with new window installations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most window installations in Pacifica, Daly City, and San Mateo County include a **manufacturer’s warranty** on the window itself and often a separate warranty on the installation work. Manufacturer warranties usually cover defects in materials, glass seal failures, and sometimes hardware for a set number of years. Installation warranties cover how the window was put in—things like flashing, sealing, and fit. It’s important to read and understand what each warranty covers and for how long. Some have different terms for glass, frames, and hardware. Many require you to maintain the window properly and may not cover damage from misuse, accidents, or major storms. Common pitfalls include assuming "lifetime" means everything is covered forever, not registering the product if required, or failing to keep records. If you sell your home, some warranties may be transferable, but others are not. If you notice problems like fogging between panes, warping, or leaks, report them as soon as possible. Waiting too long can make it harder to prove when the issue started. Keep your contract, warranty papers, and photos of the installation in a safe place so you can use them if you need service later.

description: Most window installations in Pacifica, Daly City, and San Mateo County include a **manufacturer’s warranty** on the window itself and often a separate warranty
